name.es: Vasilopita (Pan de Año Nuevo de San Basilio)
name.fr: Vasilopita (Pain du Nouvel An de Saint-Basile)

description.en: Greece's New Year loaf, cut open on January 1st for St Basil's Day with a coin baked inside for good luck. This yeasted, tsoureki-style version is scented with mahlab and mastic; many families bake a simpler cake instead.
description.de: Griechenlands Neujahrsbrot, das am 1. Januar zum Basiliusfest angeschnitten wird - mit einer Münze für Glück im neuen Jahr. Diese Hefeteig-Version im Tsoureki-Stil duftet nach Mahlab und Mastix; manche backen lieber einen Kuchen.
description.es: El pan de Año Nuevo griego, que se corta el 1 de enero por San Basilio con una moneda escondida que trae suerte. Esta versión con levadura, al estilo tsoureki, huele a mahlab y almáciga; muchas familias prefieren un bizcocho.
description.fr: Le pain du Nouvel An grec, coupé le 1er janvier pour la Saint-Basile avec une pièce porte-bonheur cachée dedans. Cette version levée façon tsoureki embaume le mahlab et le mastic ; beaucoup de familles préfèrent un gâteau.

origin.note: Named for St Basil of Caesarea, a 4th-century bishop remembered for generosity to the poor; a coin baked into the dough brings its finder luck for the coming year.

label.es: majlab (especia de hueso de cereza molido)
label.fr: mahleb (épice de noyau de cerise moulue)

ingredients.note: crushed with a little of the sugar

yield.shape: braided rope coiled into a round tin

keeping.note: Mastic and the high sugar content keep it soft for several days, unusually long for a yeasted loaf.

summary.en: Warm the milk with the sugar and butter until melted, then cool to lukewarm.
Whisk the yeast into the tepid water and let it turn foamy.
Crush the mahlab and mastic together and stir them into the flour along with the salt and orange zest.
Whisk the eggs into the cooled milk mixture, combine with the yeast water, pour over the flour, and bring together into a rough dough.
Knead with a dough hook for about 15 minutes until smooth, glossy and elastic.
Cover and let the dough double in size, roughly 2 hours.

title.en: Braid, coil and hide the coin

summary.en: Divide into three ropes, braid them, then coil the braid into a buttered tin, tucking a foil-wrapped coin into the underside of the dough. Proof again for about 2 hours.
Bake at a gentle 150°C for about 30 minutes until deep gold and hollow-sounding, then cool fully before glazing and scattering with toasted almonds.

source.adaptation: Salt raised slightly from the source's 1/2 tsp for balance. Coin custom and cake-variant note per Wikipedia's Vasilopita entry.
